summary: A diamond is a proxy contract that `delegatecall`s to multiple implementation contracts called facets. ![Diagram showing how a diamond contract works](../assets/eip-8153/basic-diamond-diagram.svg) Diamond contracts were originally standardized by [ERC-2535](./eip-2535). This ERC builds on that foundation by defining a facet-based architecture in which facets self-describe their function selectors through a standardized introspection interface. By moving selector discovery on-chain, this approach eliminates the need for off-chain selector management. As a result, diamond deployment and upgrades become simpler, more deterministic, and more gas efficient. This ERC introduces a facet introspection function, `exportSelectors()`, which every facet MUST implement. This function returns the list o
title: Facet-Based Diamonds
